content_preview.title: Solarpunk, a Two-Developer Survival Game With No Combat, Launches to 'Very Positive' Steam Reviews After a Kickstarter That Raised 10x Its Goal
content_preview.summary: Cyberwave's floating-island survival-crafting game shipped June 8 on PC and consoles, drawing favorable reviews for its weather-driven energy systems and criticism for thin late-game exploration.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Solarpunk, a first-person survival-crafting game set across a world of floating islands, released on June 8, 2026, according to its [Steam](https://store.steampowered.com/app/1805110/Solarpunk/) store page. The game is the work of Cyberwave, an indie studio described by [KeenGamer](https://www.keengamer.com/articles/reviews/solarpunk-review-a-cozy-survival-experience-in-the-sky) as having two developers, and was published by rokaplay and Metaroot, per the [Steam](https://store.steam...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight, well-structured News piece (488 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clean Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know structure. Claims are individually attributed inline to specific sources, and the article is appropriately cautious about what is and isn't known (post-launch plans, total sales).
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all five captured snapshots from disk plus a WebFetch fallback for the sixth (403-blocked) source. (1) source-0 Steam: confirmed release date '8 Jun, 2026', developer Cyberwave, publishers rokaplay/Metaroot, the classification 'survival game in a technologically advanced world of floating islands', 'construct buildings, grow food, craft gadgets', 'Alone or together with 4 friends', 'Generate electricity using wind and solar energy', 'Weather conditions affect how much energy you produce', 'Growing fruits and vegetables is your main source of food', the 'Very Positive' rating and '80%' positive figure. NOTE: snapshot shows '1,225 reviews', whereas the article quotes '1,222 reviews' (live Steam counter drift between writing and snapshot) — flagged for correction. (2) source-1 KeenGamer: confirmed 'two developers', 'There is no such thing as combat or even hunting', the no-combat-can-feel-aimless caveat, $22.99 price, and ratingValue '8' (8/10). (3) source-2 GameBrief: confirmed verbatim 'The game has zero combat. Pressure comes from energy management, food, and weather: not enemies.', 'Solar panels go dark at night. Wind turbines spin down in still air. Hydraulic generators only run near water.', 'Island variety thins past the 20-hour mark', the 'Worth buying if you want cozy logistics and don't need combat or strong narrative' conclusion, and the 7.0/10 score. (4) source-3 Kicktraq: confirmed €305,267 pledged, €30,000 goal, 6,312 backers. (5) source-4 GoNintendo: confirmed 'more than 10 times its goal' and '6,312 backers'; the source's USD figures ($330K, $32,718) were correctly NOT imported into the body, which uses Kicktraq's euro figures. (6) source-5 PS4Blog: snapshot failed with HTTP 403 (file: null in manifest), so verified via WebFetch of the live URL as a documented last resort — confirmed verbatim 'Solarpunk is out today on Nintendo Switch with a $22.99 price tag', the 7.5/10 score, and 'a first-person cozy survival game set in a technically advanced world of floating islands'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics trace to a cited source. The Switch 2 platform-availability conflict was resolved correctly: the URL slug reads 'nintendo-switch-2' but the source body says 'Nintendo Switch', and the article quotes the source's actual wording verbatim ('out today on Nintendo Switch') and only states 'A console version is also available' — it never asserts 'Switch 2' from memory or from the slug. One discrepancy: the article's direct quote '80% of the 1,222 user reviews for this game are positive' shows 1,222 where the snapshot shows 1,225; the 80% and 'Very Positive' rating both still hold. This is live-counter drift on a continuously-updating Steam figure, recoverable via a corrections note.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference. Praise and criticism are both attributed to named reviewers rather than asserted editorially.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Machine Herald coverage of the game Solarpunk or studio Cyberwave-the-game-studio (an unrelated 'Cyberwave' logistics-robotics company appears in a May 2026 article, not this developer). Not a duplicate.
concerns: Direct quote attributed to Steam reads '80% of the 1,222 user reviews for this game are positive', but the captured Steam snapshot shows '80% of the 1,225 user reviews'. Steam's review counter updates continuously; the 1,222 reflects the count when the bot wrote the article and the 1,225 the count at review-fetch time. The 80% positive figure and 'Very Positive' rating are unchanged. Filing a corrections note rather than rejecting, since the substance is intact and the issue is honestly summarizable.
recommendations: Allowlist warning is NON-BLOCKING: all six domains (store.steampowered.com, keengamer.com, gamebrief.net, kicktraq.com, gonintendo.com, ps4blog.net) are reputable for this gaming-launch story but are not in config/source_allowlist.txt. A maintainer may consider adding them; I did not edit the allowlist.
ps4blog.net bot-blocked the snapshotter (HTTP 403); the claim was verified against the live URL via WebFetch and holds. The source is valid but not auto-archivable.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate, well-sourced News piece. Headline, summary, and lead are all backed by cited sources. Every direct quote except the Steam review-count number is verbatim, and the count discrepancy is live-counter drift fully recoverable via a public corrections note. Verdict: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
